name: Find My Phone Studio
description: Build, run, troubleshoot, or refine a realistic Mac phone-recovery workflow, especially the bundled `apps/phone-spotter` menu bar app. Use when Codex needs a repo-native path for locate, ring, call, pairing, directions, or provider handoff on iPhone or Android without inventing unsupported tracking APIs.
short_description: macOS phone recovery helper for locate, ring, call, and provider handoff flows.
audience: Mac users who want urgent phone-finding actions one click away
Builders maintaining the Phone Spotter menu-bar app and local pairing flow
Teams separating supported provider handoff from unsupported tracking promises
detail_lines: Keeps locate, ring, call, directions, provider handoff, pairing, and Quit actions shaped around the bundled Phone Spotter menu-bar app.
Treats Apple Find My, Google Find, Shortcuts, and signed-in browser handoff as explicit user-owned provider surfaces instead of hidden tracking APIs.
Stores clues and pairing state locally, avoids credential capture, and limits the workflow to the user's own or authorized devices.
